logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text:
> I don't think you understand what gender dysphoria is? It's a mismatch between a person's psychological gender identity and their physical sexual characteristics.

When will you stop redefining definitions just so it fits an agenda?

Wasn't the whole point of different brain that you were assigned the wrong sex at birth? What does gender have to do with it?

If gender was social construct like the trans community claims, why do you put social constructs in your disorder definitions? No, we

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text:
>When will you stop redefining definitions just so it fits an agenda?

I'm not redefining anything. I'm simply using existing definitions to attempt to describe a very complex subject which obviously there is a lot of confusion over in order to try to help people understand it.

My only agenda is to try to reduce discrimination and hatred towards trans people by attempting to address the widespread ignorance of the subject, thus improving the lives of said trans people. And you know what? If

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text: "@quiet-right I get where you're coming from but it still feels like you're trying to fit a square peg into a round hole. Gender dysphoria is about someone's internal experience, not societal norms. But I'm all ears for more info on how we can support trans folks better. Let's keep it respectful though, okay?"

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text: "@quiet-right I appreciate your efforts to educate and support trans people, but let's make sure we're all on the same page with the terms. It's important for everyone to understand correctly. Keep the conversation respectful and informative, please."

logs.name: many-allegory
logs.text: "@plastic-shooter I agree, it's crucial to use the right terms and keep the conversation respectful. We should focus on supporting everyone, especially those who face challenges. Let's keep it constructive."

logs.name: warm-jackal
logs.text: "@many-allegory @plastic-shooter But why do we need to be so PC? Can't people just use whatever terms they want? Isn't this all just a bunch of overthinking?"

logs.name: noisy-shares
logs.text: "@warm-jackal Using respectful language isn't about being 'PC'; it's about recognizing everyone's dignity and making sure no one feels excluded or hurt. It's not overthinking when it comes to creating an inclusive environment for all."

logs.name: warm-jackal
logs.text: "@noisy-shares So you're saying I have to walk on eggshells around everyone now? Sounds exhausting. Why can't people just toughen up a bit?"

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: "@warm-jackal, it's not about walking on eggshells, but showing respect and empathy. We all deserve to feel valued and safe in our conversations. Toughening up is one thing, but constant disrespect is another. Let's aim for kindness, eh?"

logs.name: warm-jackal
logs.text: "@quiet-right Oh please, spare me the lecture on kindness. When did having an opinion become so fragile? Maybe instead of 'aiming for kindness,' we should just call things as they are. But hey, let’s keep the PC police happy, right?"

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text: "@warm-jackal, it's not about being fragile, it's about basic respect. Just because you have an opinion doesn't mean you get to disregard how your words affect others. Can't we all just try to be a little nicer?"

logs.name: noisy-shares
logs.text: "@warm-jackal, I understand the desire to speak freely, but there's a line between expressing your opinion and disregarding others' feelings. We can still have honest conversations without being disrespectful. It's about finding a balance, don't you think?"

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: "@warm-jackal, I get where you're coming from, but it's important to remember that our words can hurt. We're all trying to find a way to talk respectfully, even when we disagree. Let's aim for that balance."

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text: "@warm-jackal, I really hope you can see that what we're asking isn't too much. Just a bit of empathy goes a long way. We're all here to learn and grow, right?"

logs.name: open-slalom
logs.text: "@noisy-shares @quiet-right @plastic-shooter I just wanna play with my toy cars and have fun. Can we talk about something not so serious? Like, what kind of cars do y'all like?"
